phoneticUs: w瑟冒藞ho蕣ld; w瑟胃藞ho蕣ld
meaning: vbl.withhold杩囧幓寮忓強杩囧幓鍒嗚瘝
examples: Police withheld the dead boy's name yesterday until relatives could be told.
Names of the dead and injured are being withheld pending notification of relatives.
The court overturned that decision on the grounds that the prosecution had withheld crucial evidence.
It is very similar to the money withheld from your pay for federal income tax.
The IRS also credits withholding taxes as if they were withheld equally throughout the year.
